plan.role: You are a senior AI/ML engineer building a production-grade pipeline. Correctness first, simplicity over cleverness, no shortcuts around hard invariants. Prefer editing existing files over creating new ones. When in doubt, choose the option that is easier to test and audit.

mandatory_rules.rule: Before writing ANY code that calls agno, look up the current API with Context7 MCP (resolve-library-id -> get-library-docs). Training data is stale.
Package manager is uv. Never use pip install.
All agent output uses output_schema + Pydantic v2. Never free-text strings.
rapidfuzz runs before every LLM call — mandatory pre-filter. The translator is a pre-step BEFORE rapidfuzz, not a replacement.
Never instantiate LLM directly in agent files. Always import from infrastructure/llm/provider.py.
Do not add comments that describe WHAT the code does. Only add a comment when the WHY is non-obvious.
Do not introduce error handling for scenarios that cannot happen.
The hallucination guard in mapper_agent.py must remain untouched. The guard (canonical_title not in candidate_titles) is a hard invariant.

problem.title: Types 5, 6, 7 go to review queue because rapidfuzz candidates are wrong
problem.root_cause:
rapidfuzz compares strings character by character. For Spanish titles, abbreviations, and gender inflections, the characters are too different from O*NET titles for rapidfuzz to find correct candidates.
Result: top_score falls below low_threshold (0.70) -> band="review" -> LLM is never called. Even if we lowered the threshold, the LLM candidates would be wrong, so it would say "not equivalent" and the item would still go to review.
The fix is to normalize the input TO English BEFORE running rapidfuzz, so that rapidfuzz can find the correct O*NET candidates.
problem.evidence:
Measured scores (pre-translation): 'RRHH' -> 0.514 -> review (LLM never called, wrong candidates) 'Desarrollador Software' -> 0.600 -> review 'DBA' -> 0.600 -> review 'RN' -> 0.600 -> review
Measured scores (post-translation simulation): 'RRHH' -> translate -> 'human resources' -> 0.780 -> LLM band -> correct candidates 'DBA' -> translate -> 'database administrator'-> 0.889 -> LLM band -> "Database Administrators" 'RN' -> translate -> 'registered nurse' -> 0.849 -> LLM band -> "Registered Nurses" 'Desarrollador de Software' -> 'software developer' -> 0.865 -> LLM -> "Software Developers" 'Cientifico de Datos' -> 'data scientist' -> 0.828 -> LLM -> "Data Scientists" 'Gerenta de Marketing' -> 'marketing manager' -> 0.857 -> LLM -> "Marketing Managers"
problem.known_limits: Some items remain in review even after translation — these are genuinely ambiguous: 'CPA' -> 'certified public accountant' -> 0.611 -> still review 'Front-End Dev' -> 'frontend developer' -> 0.649 -> still review 'Full Stack Dev' -> 'full stack developer' -> 0.615 -> still review 'Talent Acquisition' -> 0.667 -> still review These are acceptable — human review is the correct outcome for them.

solution_design.overview: Add a TranslatorAgent that fires ONLY when rapidfuzz fails (band == "review"). The translator normalizes the input to English, then rapidfuzz runs again on the translated version. If the new score is better, proceed with it. The rest of the pipeline (hallucination guard, LLM band, audit) is unchanged.
solution_design.new_flow:
BEFORE (types 5-7): raw -> pre_processor -> rapidfuzz (score 0.51) -> review ❌
AFTER (types 5-7): raw -> pre_processor -> rapidfuzz (score 0.51 = review band) -> TranslatorAgent ("Desarrollador de Software" -> "software developer") -> rapidfuzz AGAIN on translated (score 0.865 = llm band) -> LLM evaluates candidates -> corrected ✅
Types 1-4 (typos, casing, seniority, noise) are unaffected: They already score >= 0.70, so the translator is never called.
solution_design.pipeline_invariants_preserved: - rapidfuzz still runs before every LLM call (now runs twice for low-score items) - Hallucination guard unchanged: canonical_title must be in candidate_titles - needs_review=True items still go to review queue — never auto-applied - TranslatorAgent output (english_title) is only used as input to rapidfuzz, never written directly to the corrected column
solution_design.translation_trigger: Translator fires ONLY when: band == "review" (top_score < 0.70). If translation does not improve the score (was_translated=False, or new score still falls in review band), the item stays in review — no extra LLM cost.

files_modified.file: agents/translator_agent.py - TranslationResult(BaseModel): english_title, was_translated, normalization_type - _translator_agent singleton - _get_agent() -> Agent - translate(raw: str) -> TranslationResult - set_agent(agent) for test injection

agents/mapper_agent.py - Import translate from translator_agent - In _decide(): after band=="review", call translate(), re-run score(), update fuzzy and band if improved - Update normalization_type in MappingDecision when translation was used
tests/test_translator.py - Unit tests for translate() with stubbed LLM
tests/test_mapper.py - Add tests for the translation path (type 5, 6, 7 cases)

current_code.file: class MappingDecision(BaseModel): raw: str preprocessed: str corrected: str | None confidence: float method: Literal["exact", "fuzzy", "llm", "needs_review"] normalization_type: Literal["typo", "synonym", "language", "format", "abbreviation", "case", "unknown"] needs_review: bool review_reason: str | None = None

@dataclass class PipelineConfig: top_n: int = 3 high_threshold: float = 0.9 low_threshold: float = 0.7 # low_threshold is the boundary between "llm" and "review" bands. # Items below low_threshold currently go straight to review. # After this plan: items below low_threshold trigger the translator first.

plan.new_file:
import logging from typing import Literal
from pydantic import BaseModel
from agno.agent import Agent
from infrastructure.llm.provider import get_model
logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
class TranslationResult(BaseModel): english_title: str was_translated: bool normalization_type: Literal["language", "abbreviation", "unknown"] = "unknown"
_translator_agent: Agent | None = None
def _get_agent() -> Agent: global _translator_agent if _translator_agent is None: _translator_agent = Agent( name="TranslatorAgent", model=get_model(), output_schema=TranslationResult, instructions=[ "You receive a job title that may be in Spanish or use abbreviations/acronyms.", "Your task: normalize it to a short English job title.", "", "Rules:", "1. Translate Spanish to English.", "2. Expand abbreviations and acronyms to full English words.", " Examples: RRHH -> Human Resources, DBA -> Database Administrator, RN -> Registered Nurse", "3. Remove gender inflection: use the neutral English form.", " Examples: Desarrolladora -> Developer, Analista Financiera -> Financial Analyst", "4. Do NOT add or remove seniority words — keep the core role only.", "5. Return ONLY the short job title — no explanations, no punctuation.", "6. If the title is already in English and needs no changes: return it unchanged with was_translated=false.", "", "Set normalization_type:", " language -> input was in a foreign language", " abbreviation -> input was an acronym or shorthand", " unknown -> cannot determine", "", "Examples:", " 'Desarrollador de Software' -> english_title='Software Developer', was_translated=true, normalization_type='language'", " 'Analista Financiera' -> english_title='Financial Analyst', was_translated=true, normalization_type='language'", " 'RRHH' -> english_title='Human Resources', was_translated=true, normalization_type='abbreviation'", " 'DBA' -> english_title='Database Administrator', was_translated=true, normalization_type='abbreviation'", " 'RN' -> english_title='Registered Nurse', was_translated=true, normalization_type='abbreviation'", " 'Software Developer' -> english_title='Software Developer', was_translated=false, normalization_type='unknown'", ], ) return _translator_agent
def translate(raw: str) -> TranslationResult: try: result = _get_agent().run(f'Job title: "{raw}"') if isinstance(result.content, TranslationResult): return result.content raise TypeError(f"Unexpected content type: {type(result.content).__name__}") except Exception as exc: logger.warning("Translation failed for %r: %s", raw, exc) return TranslationResult(english_title=raw, was_translated=False, normalization_type="unknown")
def set_agent(agent: Agent | None) -> None: """Override the agent instance. Used by tests to inject a stub.""" global _translator_agent _translator_agent = agent

step.title: API lookup — Agno Agent (Context7 MCP)
step.action:
Resolve library ID for "agno" and fetch docs for: - Agent constructor: name, model, output_schema, instructions - agent.run(prompt) return type and how to access .content
The existing agents (mapper_agent.py, audit_writer_agent.py) are the ground truth for how Agno is used in this project. Read them before writing TranslatorAgent.
The translator follows exactly the same pattern as _mapper_agent.

step.title: Create agents/translator_agent.py
step.action:
Create the file exactly as shown in the new_file section above. No deviations.
Key invariants: - translate() NEVER raises — it returns the raw input on any failure - set_agent() exists for test injection (same pattern as mapper_agent.py) - TranslationResult.english_title is always a non-empty string

step.title: Modify _decide() in agents/mapper_agent.py
step.action:
1. Add import at top of file (alongside existing imports): from agents.translator_agent import translate, TranslationResult
2. Replace the existing `if band == "review": return _review("low_confidence")` block with the translation attempt:
if band == "review": translation = translate(anomaly.raw) if translation.was_translated: fuzzy_t = score(translation.english_title, valid_categories, _CONFIG) band_t = routing_band(fuzzy_t.top_score, _CONFIG) if band_t != "review": fuzzy = fuzzy_t band = band_t if band == "review": return _review("low_confidence")
3. In the `if band == "fuzzy":` block, change normalization_type: Before: normalization_type="typo" After: normalization_type=translation.normalization_type if translation.was_translated else "typo"
BUT: `translation` is only defined if the fuzzy band was reached via translation. To handle this cleanly, initialize at the top of _decide():
translation: TranslationResult = TranslationResult( english_title=anomaly.raw, was_translated=False, normalization_type="unknown" )
Then the fuzzy block can always reference `translation.normalization_type`.
IMPORTANT: The LLM band already gets normalization_type from SemanticMatch. Do not change the LLM band logic — only the fuzzy band needs the translation type.

step.title: Create tests/test_translator.py
step.action:
Unit tests for translate() using a stubbed agent (same pattern as test_mapper.py).
Tests to include: - test_translates_spanish: "Desarrollador de Software" -> "software developer", was_translated=True - test_expands_abbreviation: "RRHH" -> "Human Resources", was_translated=True - test_expands_rn: "RN" -> "Registered Nurse", was_translated=True - test_english_unchanged: "Software Developer" -> was_translated=False - test_translation_failure_returns_raw: when LLM raises, returns raw input unchanged
Use unittest.mock.patch or set_agent() to inject a stub. No real LLM calls in unit tests.

step.title: Add translation path tests to tests/test_mapper.py
step.action:
Add tests that verify the translation path in _decide(): - test_spanish_title_reaches_llm_after_translation: Input: "Desarrollador de Software" Stub translator returns: english_title="software developer", was_translated=True Stub mapper LLM returns: is_equivalent=True, canonical_title="Software Developers" Assert: decision.corrected == "Software Developers", decision.method == "llm"
- test_abbreviation_reaches_llm_after_translation: Input: "DBA" Stub translator returns: english_title="database administrator", was_translated=True After re-score, band should be llm -> LLM called -> corrected
- test_translation_failure_goes_to_review: Stub translator returns: was_translated=False Assert: decision.needs_review=True, decision.review_reason="low_confidence"
- test_translated_but_still_low_score_goes_to_review: Stub translator returns: was_translated=True, english_title="certified public accountant" CPA translates to something that scores 0.611 -> still review Assert: decision.needs_review=True
Use set_agent() from both translator_agent and mapper_agent to inject stubs.

step.title: Run unit test suite
step.action:
uv run pytest -x -q --ignore=tests/test_integration_golden_path.py
All existing tests must pass. The translation path is additive — it only fires for band=="review" items, which existing tests stub out.

step.title: Run 100-row real LLM test and compare metrics
step.action:
uv run pytest tests/test_integration_pipeline.py::test_100_anomalies_real_llm_metrics --real-llm -v -s
Expected improvement vs baseline (before this plan): Before: Corrected=24, Review queue=76, Precision=100% After: Corrected>40, Review queue<60, Precision>=85%
The test already validates precision >= 0.85 and hallucination_rate <= 0.05. If it passes, the feature is working correctly.
Items expected to move from review to corrected after translation: DBA, RN, CS Rep, Desarrollador de Software, Cientifico de Datos, Gerenta de Marketing, Administradora de Bases de Datos, and more.
Items expected to remain in review (genuinely ambiguous): CPA, Front-End Dev, Full Stack Dev, Talent Acquisition, Xylophone Repair Technician.

do_not.item: Do NOT use the translator output (english_title) as the corrected value. It is only input to rapidfuzz. The corrected value must come from valid_categories.csv.
Do NOT call the translator for items that already reach the llm or fuzzy band. Only fire when band == "review".
Do NOT modify the hallucination guard (canonical_title not in candidate_titles).
Do NOT change MappingDecision schema fields or their Literal types — existing tests depend on the exact values.
Do NOT add error handling for cases that cannot happen. translate() already handles all LLM failures internally by returning raw input.
Do NOT create a new Agno Workflow Step for the translator. It is called directly from within _decide() as a helper function.

verification.unit_tests:
# tests/test_translator.py
from unittest.mock import MagicMock from agents.translator_agent import translate, set_agent, TranslationResult
def _make_stub(english_title: str, was_translated: bool, normalization_type: str = "unknown"): mock_agent = MagicMock() mock_result = MagicMock() mock_result.content = TranslationResult( english_title=english_title, was_translated=was_translated, normalization_type=normalization_type, ) mock_agent.run.return_value = mock_result return mock_agent
def test_translates_spanish(): set_agent(_make_stub("software developer", True, "language")) result = translate("Desarrollador de Software") assert result.english_title == "software developer" assert result.was_translated is True assert result.normalization_type == "language"
def test_expands_abbreviation(): set_agent(_make_stub("human resources", True, "abbreviation")) result = translate("RRHH") assert result.was_translated is True assert result.normalization_type == "abbreviation"
def test_english_unchanged(): set_agent(_make_stub("Software Developer", False)) result = translate("Software Developer") assert result.was_translated is False
def test_translation_failure_returns_raw(): mock_agent = MagicMock() mock_agent.run.side_effect = RuntimeError("LLM unavailable") set_agent(mock_agent) result = translate("RRHH") assert result.english_title == "RRHH" assert result.was_translated is False
verification.expected_metrics_after_implementation:
Baseline (before): Corrected: 24 / 98 anomalies Review queue: 76 Precision: 100%
Expected (after): Corrected: 40-55 / 98 anomalies (types 5-7 partially resolved) Review queue: 43-58 Precision: >= 85% (threshold from CLAUDE.md) Hallucination rate: 0% (guard unchanged)
Items that will remain in review (expected, correct behavior): - CPA (0.611 after translation — too ambiguous for O*NET) - Front-End Dev (0.649 — "Frontend Web Developers" exists but score borderline) - Full Stack Dev (0.615 — no exact O*NET equivalent) - Talent Acquisition (0.667 — borderline) - Xylophone Repair Technician (not in O*NET — correct to escalate)
